thread.title: Khofifah-Emil Achieve 68.4 Percent Electability, Predicted to Win in East Java Regional Election
thread.title_full: Khofifah-Emil Achieve 68.4 Percent Electability, Predicted to Win in East Java Regional Election

author: times news cr
title: Khofifah-Emil Achieve 68.4 Percent Electability, Predicted to Win in East Java Regional Election
text:
Thursday, 21 November 2024 ‚Äď 20:15 WIB
Candidate for Governor-Deputy‚ÄĆ Governor of East Java serial number 2 Khofifah Indar Parawansa -Emil Elestianto ‚ÄĆDardak. Photo:‚Äč Source for JPNN.com.
jpnn.com ‚Äď JAKARTA ‚Äď The Indonesian Poltracking Institute released the results of the latest ‚ĀĘsurvey on ‚ÄĆthe ‚Äčelectability of candidate pairs in the 2024 ‚ÄčEast Java Regional Election.‚Ā§
The East Java Governor-Deputy Governor Candidate pair‚ÄĆ number 2 ‚Ā§Khofifah Indar‚Äč Parawansa-Emil Elestianto Dardak, has the potential to‚Ā£ win the 2024 East Java Regional Election.
Khofifah-Emil achieved 68.4 percent electability, far ahead of her rivals.
These findings refer to a survey conducted in the period‚Äć 13-19‚Äć November 2024 using a stratified multistage random sampling‚Ā£ method and a ‚ĀĘsample of 2,000 respondents. If the survey results are combined ‚Äćplus and minus with a margin of‚ÄĆ error ‚ĀĘof ‚Äč2.2 percent, it is found ‚ÄĆthat the lower limit is 66.2 ‚ĀĘpercent and the upper limit is 70.6‚ÄĆ percent.
‚ÄúWe predict‚ÄĆ that ‚ÄĆthe ‚Ā§winner of the 2024 East Java Governor Election will be candidate number 2 Khofifah Indar Parawansa-Emil Elestianto Dardak ‚Ā§with a score of around 66.2 to 70.6 percent,‚ÄĚ said‚ĀĘ the Executive ‚ÄĆDirector of Poltracking Indonesia, Hanta Yuda, in an online survey release presentation. , Thursday (21/11).
He‚Ā£ also explained that in second place there was the pair Tri Rismaharini-Zahrul‚Äč Azhar or Gus Hans with an electability of 24.2 percent. Followed by Luluk‚Ā£ Nur Hamidah-Lukmanul Khakim in third position with an electability of 3.8 percent.
Hanta said ‚Äčthat the high voter ‚ĀĘparticipation rate in the 2024‚ÄĆ East Java ‚ĀĘgubernatorial election also played a key role. Especially in predicting survey results which up to now have been recorded ‚ĀĘas still being outperformed by the incumbent pair.
‚ÄúOf course, this‚Ā§ figure ‚Äćis also related‚Ā§ to the relatively stable level of participation, meaning that at 70‚Ā§ or 80 to 90 percent, it is possible that this survey figure is not that far‚Ā£ off,‚ÄĚ he said.
The results of the ‚Ā§Indonesian Poltracking survey, Khofifah Indar Parawansa-Emil Elestianto Dardak, have the potential ‚ĀĘto win ‚Äćthe 2024 East‚Äć Java regional elections.

How does the electability of candidates in ‚Äćthe Poltracking survey reflect voter demographics and preferences? Interview: Time.news Editor with Hanta Yuda, Executive Director of Poltracking Indonesia
Editor: Good afternoon, Hanta! Thank you ‚Ā§for joining us today. I‚Äôm excited to discuss the recent Poltracking‚ĀĘ survey ‚Äčresults regarding the East Java Regional Election. Let‚Äôs dive into your findings. Khofifah Indar Parawansa and Emil Elestianto Dardak seem to have a significant lead with an impressive 68.4% ‚Ā§electability. What do you think contributed to their strong position‚Äč in this election?
Hanta Yuda: Good afternoon! Thanks for having me. Yes,‚Äć Khofifah and ‚ÄćEmil‚Äôs substantial‚ÄĆ electability can be attributed to several factors. Firstly, Khofifah‚ÄĆ has a strong track record as a leader, having previously served as the Governor of East Java, and she has built ‚Ā£a solid ‚Äčreputation. Moreover, Emil‚Äôs dynamic approach as a young leader ‚ĀĘresonates with ‚ÄĆa wider demographic, especially younger voters. ‚Ā§Their combined experience and public image have created a compelling narrative that appeals to the electorate.
Editor: ‚Ā§Fascinating! Your survey used a stratified multistage random sampling method with 2,000 respondents. Can you explain how this methodology works and why it is beneficial for achieving accurate results?
Hanta Yuda: Absolutely! Stratified multistage random sampling is a method‚Äć that ensures we capture a‚Äć diverse range ‚Äćof perspectives‚Ā§ within the population. We divide the population into different ‚ĀĘsubgroups or strata based on characteristics such as age, gender, and geography.‚Äć Then, we randomly sample from each subgroup.‚Ā§ This process helps to minimize bias and enhances the representativeness ‚ÄĆof our findings, which‚Äč is crucial ‚Äćfor accurately predicting election outcomes.
Editor: That makes a lot of‚Ā£ sense. Now, you ‚Äčmentioned that while Khofifah ‚Äćand Emil are leading, the second candidate pair, Tri Rismaharini and Zahrul Azhar, lag behind ‚Äćwith only 24.2% electability. What do you‚Ā£ think are the reasons for ‚Äčtheir lower standing in this‚Ā£ election?
Hanta Yuda: Tri Rismaharini, despite her notable experience‚ÄĆ as the Mayor of Surabaya, may be facing‚Äć challenges in ‚Ā£broadening her appeal beyond urban voters. Our survey indicates that Khofifah and ‚ĀĘEmil‚Äôs message resonates more strongly‚Ā£ across ‚Äćvarious demographics.‚Ā£ Additionally, there may be perceptions about their‚Ā§ ability to deliver on key issues that matter to ‚Ā§voters, such as economic recovery and infrastructure development, which Khofifah and Emil are addressing more effectively.
Editor: Interesting points! You also ‚Äčdiscussed the importance of‚ÄĆ voter participation in this election. How do you think that will influence the final outcome?
Hanta Yuda: Voter participation is indeed pivotal. High turnout rates typically favor‚Ā§ candidates who have ‚Äća more robust grassroots campaign and connect effectively‚Ā§ with the public. If we see high participation rates, it could solidify Khofifah and Emil‚Äôs lead, as their campaigns have been designed to engage directly with the voters. Conversely, lower ‚ĀĘturnout could open the door for surprises, but currently, the data suggests ‚Äćthat they are well-positioned.
Editor: It sounds ‚Ā£like there are a lot ‚Äčof dynamics‚Ā£ at play. looking ahead, what are your predictions for the‚ÄĆ remaining‚Ā£ days leading up‚Ā£ to the election?
Hanta Yuda: Based on the current data, I would cautiously predict that Khofifah and Emil will maintain their lead, barring any ‚Ā§significant shifts in public sentiment or unforeseen‚Ā£ developments. ‚ÄĆHowever, as we know, politics can be unpredictable. It‚Äôs essential for candidates to continue to engage with voters and address any concerns that arise. The final weeks will be critical for ‚ĀĘall candidates to solidify their positions.
Editor: ‚ÄčThank you for sharing your insights, ‚Ā£Hanta. It‚Äôs enlightening to delve into the nuances of‚Ā§ the ‚ĀĘelection process and voter sentiment. We look forward to seeing how the results unfold!
Hanta Yuda: ‚Äć Thank you for having me! It‚Äôs always‚ÄĆ a pleasure‚Ā£ to discuss ‚Äćthese important‚Äć topics. Let‚Äôs keep a close eye on the election as it approaches.
